title: Fiona Houser
text:
My name is Fiona Houser, and this is my personal website/portfolio made for GEOG 490. I am a fourth year student at the University of Oregon, double majoring in Spatial Data Science (SDS) and Spanish. I have completed course work using Python, CSS, C, Javascript, HTML, R, RStudio, Mapbox, and ArcGIS Online.
In the case study I perform, linked above, I analyze demographic change over time in the Portland-Vancouver-Hillsboro core-based statistical area (CBSA). To create the figures shown, I used RStudio to work with census data, analyzing and visualizing specific variables, like an age and sex population breakdown,
I previously worked as an AI/Tech intern at SunshineOxygen in Barcelona, Spain. I spent much of my time at the company developing a generative AI algorithm (GAIA) that produced synthetic hydrology and weather data. This GAIA was used to create a powerful model for disaster forecasting and event planning.
I have completed mapping projects using ArcGIS, R and RStudio, as I am interested in this mapping research subject and this mapping research subject. I have completed research projects in XY and Z. I hope to work in XY and Z.
To learn more about Quarto websites visit https://quarto.org/docs/websites.

title: Case Study on Portland, OR
text:
In this section you can see the culmination of all that I have learned in this class. I perform a case study on demographic change in my hometown, Portland, OR. Specifically I look at the entire CBSA, which spans two states (Oregon and Washington) and has a population of approximately 2.5 million people.



Here is a view of the census tracts in the Portland-Vancouver-Hillsboro CBSA


There are 568 census tracts in the CBSA.
